title: Thread-scope pattern for sync AWS client construction is correct
description: AwsSmBackend::from_uri (and other AWS backends) uses std::thread::scope to spin up a short-lived tokio runtime for aws_config::load_from_env (core lib:89-109, aws-sm lib:74-99). A contributor might suggest 'just use block_on' or 'require an async constructor'. Reason to resist: block_on panics if called from within an existing tokio runtime (the common case). An async constructor forces all call sites to be async. The scoped thread approach handles both contexts correctly with no impact on call sites. The scoped borrow guarantees the thread is joined before returning, so no unsafe transmute of lifetimes is needed.
